notes.dedup_methodology: Aggressive dedup: exact duplicates, substring/prefix matches, typo variants of short messages (continue/contionue/contonue), progressive expansions where user typed more before re-sending, and local-command-stdout system responses (not user messages). 5 /exit commands collapsed to 1 unique since they are identical.

category_definitions.continuation: Just 'continue', 'go ahead', 'yes', acknowledgments to keep going
category_definitions.build-ide: Instructions about building the IDE harness, hooks, tools, orient command, pain-check system, self-reflection automation
category_definitions.build-app: Instructions about building the Figma clone features: renderer, WASM, imports, UI tools, stress tests, specific artboards, drawing, parity with Figma
category_definitions.correction: Pointing out bugs, hallucinations, wrong approaches, broken features, regressions
category_definitions.strategic-steering: Asking agent to think, reflect, plan, step back, consider pain points, evaluate progress, question goals
category_definitions.context-management: About compaction, memory tools, context window, how agent manages its own state across sessions
category_definitions.other: Messages that don't fit other categories

key_observations: 32% of unique messages are build-app (the actual Figma clone work)
19% are strategic-steering (user constantly pulling agent back to think before coding)
16% are build-ide (the meta-project of building tools for the AI itself)
6% are context-management (recurring frustration about memory tool overwriting)
The user intervened repeatedly despite stating they would not, suggesting the agent struggled with autonomous operation
A recurring theme: the agent kept using memory tools it had no control over instead of IDE tools it built for itself
28% duplicate rate reflects the stop-and-resend pattern plus progressive message expansion
